channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: ICE and DOGE seek sensitive data in crackdown on illegal immigration, waste: report
item.description: As the Trump administration cracks down on illegal immigration and government waste, ICE and DOGE reportedly seek sensitive data allegedly to track down individuals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota license centers see long wait times as Real ID deadline nears
item.description: Real ID deadlines came and went for years. But now, with the actual deadline next month, people who weren't sure if the requirement would ever be implemented are now waiting hours to get their new IDs.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: HHS probing hospital over firing of nurse who blew whistle on minors getting gender treatments
item.description: HHS is investigating a hospital after a nurse who sought a religious exemption to avoid administering puberty blockers and cross-sex hormones to children was fired.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Radical activist who fueled Cori Bush's campaigns emerges as spokesperson for track star's murder suspect
item.description: A spokesperson for the family of the 17-year-old boy accused of stabbing a teen track star to death has a long history of supporting defunding the police as well as close ties to a former 'Squad' Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas hemp farmers fear full THC ban would nip industry in the bud
item.description: As lawmakers work to close loopholes that allow minors access to cannabis consumables, hemp farmers warn a total THC ban would strangle a once-hailed crop.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Stephen Simpson, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump: China calling ‘a lot’ since last week's tariff increase, deal could happen as soon as 3 weeks
item.description: Reporters ask President Trump about the status of his negotiations with China and, in particular, whether he has spoken to Chinese President Xi Jinping amid the ongoing trade war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Law firms, universities and now civil society groups are in Trump's sights for punitive action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — First the nation’s top law firms. Then its premier universities. Now, President Donald Trump is leaning on the advocacy groups that underpin U.S. civil society.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration seeks explosive expansion of nation's immigration detention system
item.description: The world's largest immigration detention system is on the cusp of explosive growth as President Donald Trump pursues his signature campaign promise of mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Sara Cline And Kate Brumback, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ethics investigation of state Senate president prompts conflict-of-interest rules scrutiny
item.description: Attorney and former State DFL House Majority Leader Ryan Winkler joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition on Thursday to talk about conflicts of interest at the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: The Abrego Garcia case pulls Democrats into the immigration debate Trump wants to have
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For Democrats, the Kilmar Abrego Garcia case is about fundamental American ideals — due process, following court orders, preventing government overreach. For the Trump administration and Republicans, it's about foreigners and gang threats and danger in American towns and cities.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: HUD puts half-occupied headquarters building in DC up for sale
item.description: HUD's headquarters was put on a list of federal assets to be sold, and it is facing hundreds of millions in deferred maintenance costs with only half the building being used.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP and the Trump administration argue over presidential access before appeals judges
item.description: The Associated Press and the Trump administration returned to a courtroom Thursday — and will be in another on Friday — as part of the high-stakes battle over who can control which journalists are able to question the president. Lawyers argued before a three-judge panel of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge will wait to rule on an effort to block Trump's executive order on elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday said she would wait to decide whether to grant a preliminary injunction to national Democrats and voting rights groups that are challenging President Donald Trump’s recent executive order on elections. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Digging into Minnesota‘s political history
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and a team of MPR News reporters and producers share a bit Minnesota political history with some modern twists.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Letitia James reaps what she sows after leveling 'laughable' case at Trump: expert
item.description: A federal housing agency sent a criminal referral to the Department of Justice Monday arguing New York Attorney General Letitia James appeared to have falsified mortgage records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump admin guts AmeriCorps, Clinton-era volunteer agency that failed 8 consecutive audits
item.description: The Trump administration placed roughly 75% of full-time AmeriCorps employees on administrative leave Wednesday, Fox News Digital learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moderate Republicans draw red line on Medicaid cuts in Trump agenda bill
item.description: Vulnerable and moderate House Republicans are warning House GOP leaders that they will not support the “one big, beautiful bill” encompassing President Trump’s legislative agenda if it includes cuts to Medicaid benefits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Florida House votes to stop pickleball courts, hotels from being built in state parks
item.description: A bill aiming to protect Florida's state parks from overdevelopment was unanimously passed Wednesday by the state House.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York AG referred to DOJ for alleged mortgage fraud
item.description: If proven, the claims could lead to prosecution for one of New York’s highest-ranking officials, who has long argued that “no one is above the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Mississippi River named 'most endangered' by conservation group, cites FEMA cuts
item.description: The organization said the federal government plays a key role in protecting the river and helping homeowners prepare for, and rebuild after, major flooding.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: White House invites Rachel Morin's mother to press briefing to share daughter's story
item.description: The mother of a Maryland woman who was killed by an individual who entered the country illegally addressed reporters at the White House on Wednesday as the Trump administration seeks to go on offense over the case of an individual who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Vance to visit leaders of Italy, India
item.description: Vice President Vance will travel next week to Italy and India to meet with leaders there and visit cultural sites. Vance will be joined by second lady Usha Vance and their children for the trip, which will begin Friday with a trip to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Biden, Obama-appointed judges oversee bulk of Trump cases
item.description: The Gavel is The Hill's courts newsletter. Sign up by clicking here or adding your email in the box below.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Judge: 'Probable cause' to hold U.S. in contempt over Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The government sent several planeloads of alleged gang members to El Salvador, including 137 people under the act, the White House said at the time. The judge gave the U.S. until April 23 to respond.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: DOJ suing Maine amid Title IX, trans athlete dispute
item.description: Maine's governor says a state law barring discrimination based on gender identity supersedes Trump's executive order.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Oler, NewsNation

item.title: Keri Heintzeman wins Republican primary in race to fill Justin Eichorn’s state Senate seat
item.description: The winner of the April 29 special election will fill the District 6 seat left vacant by Justin Eichorn, who resigned last month after he was charged with soliciting a minor.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: NIL payments OK at UNLV, UNR, but payment amounts secret under SB293
item.description: A bill concerning NIL — name, image, likeness — and how UNLV and UNR can manage contracts directly with players passed on Tuesday in the Nevada Senate, advancing to the Assembly with other bills. A Republican leader objected to secrecy surrounding the payment amounts.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: States push Medicaid work rules, but few programs help enrollees find jobs
item.description: Some lawmakers are pushing to require that Medicaid recipients work in order to get or keep coverage, and some states already try to help them find jobs. But the effects of those efforts are unclear.
item.dc:creator: Sam Whitehead, Phil Galewitz, and Katheryn Houghton, MPR News

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: A whistleblower’s disclosure details how DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data
item.description: A whistleblower tells Congress and NPR that DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data and hid its tracks. “None of that information should ever leave the agency,” said a former NLRB official.
item.dc:creator: Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Pritzker says he's undecided about testifying before Congressional panel
item.description: The US House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform requested testimony from Pritzker, other Democrats about sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Trump admin tells judge man wrongly deported to El Salvador is alive, still detained
item.description: On Friday, federal judge Paula Xinis had ordered the Trump administration to provide daily updates to return Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Florida education bill sparks debate over funds for advanced placement programs
item.description: Florida lawmakers battled it out on the House floor this week over a bill that could lead to significant funding cuts to education.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5
